commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9900f16802058768dc0ae592ba137119046f8d6b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR snapshots active global-prefix OpenClaw host dependencies around managed npm plugin installs, repairs the active host package at the same version when dependencies disappear, rolls back on repair failure, and adds focused npm-root tests.

PR surface: Source +227, Tests +216. Total +443 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is present. Source inspection and the PR's modeled regression tests make the current-main gap clear, but the affected real global-prefix npm install path was not exercised here.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because this draft protected-label PR needs the verifier fix and real global-prefix npm proof before merge; automation cannot supply the contributor's environment proof.

target.title: Repair active npm host after plugin installs
target.body:
Summary
- Detect when a managed plugin npm install prunes dependencies from the active global-prefix OpenClaw package.
- Repair the active OpenClaw package at the same installed version before reporting plugin install success, including required dependencies and optional runtime dependencies that were present before the install.
- Roll back plugin installs if active-host repair fails, and keep npm lifecycle scripts disabled during repair.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/npm-managed-root.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/plugins/install.ts src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts
- git diff --check

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Installing an external plugin into the same managed npm prefix as a global OpenClaw package can leave the active OpenClaw package missing runtime dependencies.
Real environment tested: Focused local regression tests that model a global-prefix OpenClaw package and a managed plugin install that removes active host dependencies.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.npm-spec.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts
Evidence after fix: The regression verifies the plugin install repairs the active OpenClaw package at the same version and restores both required dependencies and previously present optional dependencies.
Observed result after fix: 2 test files passed, 58 tests passed.
What was not tested: A live npm registry install/update on a production gateway; this PR keeps proof local and scoped to the npm managed-root regression.
target.author: mbelinky
